title: OCR Error Detection Hotfix
body:
Fix input handling of error detection model: 
- Segment length is automatically handled by tokenizer, and is by `#tokens`, not `#chars`, removing the splitting logic inside `marker`
- Join lines using `\n`

step_plan.summary: Remove trailing newlines from OCR-built Spans and change the LLM text processor to supply a single '\n'-joined text block to the prompt while retaining the per-line list for validation, preventing duplicate newlines and relying on tokenizer token limits rather than char-based splitting.

steps.operation: Remove trailing newline when creating Spans
steps.reason:
Spans are currently created with text = fix_text(ocr_line.text) + "
", which causes duplicate/newline proliferation when downstream processors (LLMTextProcessor) join lines with '
' or append their own trailing newlines. Removing the concatenated "
" keeps each Span's text as the normalized OCR text, delegating line-joining to downstream processors and the model/tokenizer.
steps.side_effects: Any code or tests that expect Span.text to include a trailing '\n' will break; update tests and any dependent code that relied on the trailing newline. Downstream logic that previously relied on char-based splitting may see slightly different string contents; however, model tokenization and token-based splitting remain unaffected. Persisted span representations (DB/serialized forms) will change if stored with trailing newlines; migrations/tests may be needed.

steps.operation: Join per-line texts with '\n' when building LLM prompt while retaining list for validation
steps.reason: Instead of inserting a JSON array of per-line strings or sending lines separately, build a single joined string extracted_lines_joined = '\n'.join(extracted_lines_list) for the {extracted_lines} placeholder in the prompt template. Keep extracted_lines_list (the list of lines) to validate that len(corrected_lines) == len(extracted_lines_list). This implements the PR requirement to join lines using '\n' and avoids per-character splitting inside marker.
steps.side_effects: Prompt shape changes: LLM receives a single newline-joined block instead of a list-like JSON payload; prompts and model behavior should be tested to ensure no unintended tokenization/formatting differences. Validation logic remains the same, so API-level behavior (response schema) should be stable. Unit/integration tests that assert on the exact prompt text or on JSON-wrapped extracted lines must be updated. Ensure the downstream step that materializes corrected_lines into Spans still appends a single trailing '\n' per created span (preserve existing behavior) to avoid losing line breaks in final output.
